name: Biomedical Engineering
description: Biomedical engineering applies engineering principles and design concepts to medicine and biology for healthcare purposes. It bridges engineering and medical sciences to develop diagnostic devices, therapeutic equipment, and rehabilitation technologies that improve patient care and quality of life.

subjects.name: Human Anatomy & Physiology
subjects.description: Comprehensive study of the structure and function of human body systems including skeletal, muscular, nervous, cardiovascular, respiratory, and digestive systems. Emphasizes the relationship between anatomical structure and physiological function relevant to medical device design.
learning_outcomes: Identify major anatomical structures and describe their physiological functions
Explain the interrelationships between body systems in maintaining homeostasis
Apply knowledge of human physiology to design constraints for medical devices
Analyze physiological signals in the context of diagnostic instrumentation
subjects.industry_relevance: Foundational for designing medical devices that interact safely and effectively with the human body.

subjects.name: Biomedical Instrumentation
subjects.description: Design and operating principles of medical instruments used for diagnosis and monitoring including ECG, EEG, EMG, blood pressure monitors, pulse oximeters, and ventilators. Covers signal acquisition, amplification, filtering, and safety standards.
learning_outcomes: Design biopotential amplifiers for ECG, EEG, and EMG signal acquisition
Apply medical safety standards including leakage current and patient isolation
Calibrate and maintain common diagnostic and monitoring instruments
Analyze sources of artifact and noise in biomedical measurements
subjects.industry_relevance: Core skill set for medical device companies manufacturing patient monitoring and diagnostic equipment.

subjects.name: Biomaterials
subjects.description: Study of natural and synthetic materials used in medical applications including metals, ceramics, polymers, and composites. Covers biocompatibility, host response, sterilization, and material selection for implants and medical devices.
learning_outcomes: Classify biomaterials and describe their mechanical and biological properties
Evaluate biocompatibility through in vitro and in vivo testing methods
Select appropriate materials for orthopedic, cardiovascular, and dental implants
Analyze degradation mechanisms of biomaterials in physiological environments
subjects.industry_relevance: Essential for implant manufacturing, wound care products, and drug delivery system development.

subjects.name: Biomechanics
subjects.description: Application of classical mechanics to biological systems including analysis of forces in musculoskeletal system, joint mechanics, gait analysis, and mechanical behavior of biological tissues. Covers stress-strain analysis of bone, cartilage, and soft tissues.
learning_outcomes: Apply free-body diagrams and static analysis to musculoskeletal systems
Analyze joint reaction forces and moments during human movement
Characterize the mechanical properties of bone, cartilage, and soft tissues
Perform gait analysis using force plates and motion capture systems
subjects.industry_relevance: Fundamental for orthopedic implant design, sports equipment development, and rehabilitation robotics.

subjects.name: Medical Imaging Systems
subjects.description: Principles and instrumentation of medical imaging modalities including X-ray, CT, MRI, ultrasound, and nuclear medicine. Covers image formation, reconstruction algorithms, image quality assessment, and radiation safety.
learning_outcomes: Explain the physical principles behind X-ray, CT, MRI, and ultrasound imaging
Analyze image reconstruction algorithms including filtered back projection
Evaluate image quality metrics including resolution, contrast, and SNR
Apply radiation safety principles for X-ray and nuclear medicine modalities
subjects.industry_relevance: Core knowledge for roles in medical imaging companies, radiology departments, and imaging research.

subjects.name: Biosensors
subjects.description: Design and development of biosensors for medical diagnostics including electrochemical, optical, piezoelectric, and thermal sensors. Covers bioreceptor immobilization, signal transduction, and applications in point-of-care testing and wearable health monitoring.
learning_outcomes: Design enzyme-based and affinity-based biosensors for analyte detection
Apply electrochemical and optical transduction principles in biosensor development
Evaluate biosensor performance parameters including sensitivity, selectivity, and LOD
Develop wearable and implantable biosensor systems for continuous health monitoring
subjects.industry_relevance: Rapidly growing field with applications in diabetes management, infectious disease testing, and fitness tracking.

subjects.name: Rehabilitation Engineering
subjects.description: Application of engineering to assist individuals with disabilities including assistive technologies, wheelchair design, communication aids, and rehabilitation robotics. Covers user-centered design, functional electrical stimulation, and outcome assessment.
learning_outcomes: Design assistive devices using user-centered design methodology
Apply functional electrical stimulation for motor restoration in paralysis
Evaluate rehabilitation outcomes using standardized assessment tools
Develop control strategies for powered exoskeletons and prosthetic limbs
subjects.industry_relevance: Growing demand due to aging population and increased focus on accessibility and quality of life.

subjects.name: Artificial Organs
subjects.description: Design and development of artificial organs including artificial heart, ventricular assist devices, artificial kidney, artificial pancreas, and cochlear implants. Covers blood-material interactions, implantable power systems, and clinical evaluation.
learning_outcomes: Design ventricular assist devices considering hemodynamics and blood compatibility
Analyze mass transport in artificial kidney and hemodialysis systems
Evaluate biocompatibility and thrombosis risks in blood-contacting artificial organs
Understand regulatory pathways for implantable artificial organ approval
subjects.industry_relevance: Critical for medical device companies developing life-saving implantable organ support systems.

subjects.name: Neural Engineering
subjects.description: Interface between engineering and neuroscience covering neural recording and stimulation, brain-computer interfaces, neural prosthetics, and neuromodulation. Covers electrode design, neural signal processing, and closed-loop neural control.
learning_outcomes: Design microelectrode arrays for neural recording and stimulation
Develop signal processing algorithms for decoding neural activity in brain-computer interfaces
Apply neuromodulation techniques including deep brain stimulation and transcranial magnetic stimulation
Evaluate the performance of neural prosthetics in restoring sensory or motor function
subjects.industry_relevance: Rapidly advancing field with applications in neuroprosthetics, deep brain stimulation, and cognitive enhancement.

subjects.name: Clinical Engineering
subjects.description: Management of medical technology in healthcare settings including equipment acquisition, maintenance, risk management, and technology assessment. Covers hospital information systems, medical device regulations, and healthcare technology management.
learning_outcomes: Develop medical equipment management programs including preventive maintenance and calibration
Apply risk management frameworks for medical device safety in clinical settings
Evaluate health technology for procurement decisions using multi-criteria analysis
Understand medical device regulations including FDA, CE marking, and ISO 13485
subjects.industry_relevance: Essential for hospital technology management and medical device regulatory compliance.

subjects.name: Hospital Management
subjects.description: Management principles applied to healthcare organizations including hospital administration, financial management, quality improvement, patient safety, and healthcare information systems. Covers hospital accreditation and healthcare policy.
learning_outcomes: Apply management principles to hospital operations and resource allocation
Develop quality improvement programs using Lean and Six Sigma methodologies
Analyze healthcare financial statements and budgeting for hospital departments
Evaluate patient safety initiatives and hospital accreditation requirements
subjects.industry_relevance: Growing need for professionals who understand both engineering and healthcare administration.

subjects.name: Biomedical Signal Processing
subjects.description: Digital signal processing techniques applied to biomedical signals including ECG, EEG, EMG, and PPG. Covers filtering, feature extraction, time-frequency analysis, pattern recognition, and machine learning for diagnostic decision support.
learning_outcomes: Apply digital filters to remove noise and artifacts from biomedical signals
Extract clinically relevant features from ECG, EEG, and EMG signals
Implement time-frequency analysis using wavelet and STFT methods
Develop machine learning classifiers for automated diagnostic decision support
subjects.industry_relevance: Core technology for wearable health monitors, diagnostic devices, and remote patient monitoring systems.

subjects.name: Medical Ethics
subjects.description: Ethical principles in healthcare and biomedical research including patient autonomy, informed consent, confidentiality, end-of-life decisions, and research ethics. Covers ethical issues in genetic testing, clinical trials, and emerging medical technologies.
learning_outcomes: Apply ethical frameworks including principlism to healthcare dilemmas
Analyze informed consent requirements in clinical care and research
Evaluate ethical issues in genetic testing, reproductive technology, and organ transplantation
Understand regulatory requirements for ethical conduct of clinical trials
subjects.industry_relevance: Required knowledge for IRB membership, clinical trial design, and healthcare policy development.

subjects.name: Prosthetics & Orthotics
subjects.description: Design and fitting of prosthetic limbs and orthotic devices including upper and lower extremity prosthetics, spinal orthoses, and modern microprocessor-controlled prostheses. Covers socket design, materials, gait training, and outcome measurement.
learning_outcomes: Design prosthetic sockets considering pressure distribution and suspension principles
Apply microprocessor-controlled knee and ankle systems for transfemoral prostheses
Evaluate orthotic interventions for spinal, upper limb, and lower limb conditions
Assess prosthetic and orthotic outcomes using functional and quality-of-life measures
subjects.industry_relevance: Directly applicable to the prosthetic and orthotic industry serving millions of amputees and mobility-impaired individuals.
